zh.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V於大約1123年出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 斯塔恩格安德斯。他和Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1188年12月14日在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 藍斯格 阿姆 萊赫迪斯 阿姆 阿莫瑟享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V于大约1123年出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 斯塔恩格安德斯。他和Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1188年12月14日在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 藍斯格 阿姆 萊赫迪斯 阿姆 阿莫瑟享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English duk(ke), duck, doke, dook ‘duck’ (Old English dūce), either from a perceived resemblance (perhaps a waddling gait) or from association with wild fowling. Compare Duck , Drake .

English: from the Middle English personal name Duk or Duke. In northern England this is usually a pet form of Marmaduke. It may alternatively be a survival of one or more Old English personal names, though it is uncertain whether they were still current in the period of surname formation. Old English Ducc(a) is attested in placenames like Duxford (Cambridgeshire) and Duckington (Cheshire), and was perhaps interchangeable with Docc, attested in Doxey (Staffordshire) and Doxford (Northumberland). Duke could also represent Old English Deowuc (as in Deuxhill, Shropshire). A surname from Marmaduke is on record until at least 1881 and derives from the personal name Marmaduke, apparently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Old Irish Maolmaedóc ‘devotee of Maedóc’; see Duckett .
Americanized form of Polish Duk: nickname from dukać ‘to stammer or falter’.

variants.name: Dock
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Duke  .
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm named Dokk, from Old Norse dǫkk ‘hollow, depression’.
North German (Lower Saxony): possibly a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in the cloth trade, from Middle Low German dōk ‘fabric’.

variants.name: Doke
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .

variants.name: Duch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Nga, Phal, Phan, Sophan, Tha, Thang, Yen.  German Kurt, Klaus.
Polish, Sorbian, and Ukrainian: nickname from duch ‘spirit, breath’.
Czech: from a short form of the Old Czech personal name Duchoslav, composed of the elements duch ‘spirit’ + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Dukes
meanings: English: variant of Duke , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Meran,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Ricardo, Alfredo, Javier, Julio, Raul.
Spanish (Terán): habitational name from Terán in Cantabria.
Slovenian: probably a nickname derived from terati ‘to push; to torture’.

nameMeanings.name: Count
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earl
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Margrave
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Wolf.

German and Dutch: status name from Middle High German marcgrāve ‘margrave’ (which is a title derived from marc ‘boundary’ + grāve ‘royal judge’). In some cases the title may have been applied as a nickname for someone with overbearing behavior or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a margrave.

variants.name: Margraf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Mathias.
German and Dutch (of German origin): variant of   Markgraf  .

nameMeanings.name: Berthold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: berht ‘bright’ + hold ‘splendid’; alteration of earlier Bertwald, from berht + wald ‘rule, power’.

variants.name: Barthold
meanings: form of   Berthold .

en.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V was born about 1123, in Andechs, Starnberg, Bavaria, Germany. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. He died on 14 December 1188, in Dießen am Ammersee, Landsberg am Lech, Bavaria, Germany, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V est né environ 1123, à Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Allemagne. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. Il est décédé le 14 décembre 1188, à Dießen am Ammersee, Landkreis Landsberg am Lech, Bayern, Allemagne, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V wurde um 1123 in Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren. Er hatte mit Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Dezember 1188 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Dießen am Ammersee, Landkreis Landsberg am Lech, Bayern, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V è nato il circa 1123 nel Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Germania. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. Morì il 14 dicembre 1188, nel Dießen am Ammersee, Landkreis Landsberg am Lech, Bayern, Germania, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria Vは約1123年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス スターンバーグアンデチスで生まれた。彼はHedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbachとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1188年12月14日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ランドスバーグ アム レチディエssン アム アンメルセで66歳で死去した。

ko.Dießen am Ammersee, Landsberg am Lech, B: 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 랜드스버그 아 렉h 디ss 아 아머시
ko.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V(은)는 대략 1123년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 스타른버그 앤데크스에서 태어났다. 그는 Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1188년 12월 14일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 랜드스버그 아 렉h 디ss 아 아머시에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V nasceu aproximadamente 1123, em Andechs, Landkreis Starnberg, Bayern, Alemanha. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. Ele faleceu em 14 de dezembro de 1188, em Dießen am Ammersee, Landkreis Landsberg am Lech, Bayern, Alemanha, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Berthold Duke Of Meran, Count Of Andech, Innsbruck, & Wolfrathausen, Margrave Of Istria V родился около 1123 г. в местности андечс, ландкрис старнбирг, беиирн, Германия. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. Он умер 14 декабря 1188 г. в местности диеssен ам аммирси, ландкрис ландсбирг ам леч, беиирн, Германия в возрасте 66 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Hedwig von Scheyern-Dachau Wittelsbach. Murió el 14 de diciembre de 1188, en Dießen am Ammersee, Landkreis Landsberg am Lech, Bayern, Alemania, a la edad de 66 años.
